user.title: VoIP Stack
user.description: SIP endpoint, bridge/router and local registrar for ESPHome VoIP phones. HA listens on SIP UDP and TCP automatically.

data.debug_mode: Enable SIP/RTP debug and private audio captures
data.experimental_sip_video: Enable SIP video for the Home Assistant softphone

data_description.sip_port: Listen port for the HA SIP endpoint. HA accepts SIP signaling over both UDP and TCP on this port.
data_description.rtp_port: Base RTP UDP port for HA media and relays.
data_description.advertise_host: Optional override published in ESP phonebooks. Use this when HA is behind routing, NAT, LXC, VPN, or multiple subnets and the automatically announced address is not reachable by ESP devices.
data_description.assist_intents: Register optional Assist handlers for call, hang up, answer and decline ("Hey Jarvis, call Kitchen"). Spoken contact names are resolved dynamically against the live VoIP phonebook.
data_description.assist_endpoint_enabled: Add a SIP extension backed directly by a Home Assistant Assist pipeline. No separate VoIP integration or SIP listener is required.
data_description.debug_mode: Enables detailed SIP/RTP/media diagnostics and logs. It also records private call audio automatically: up to 15 seconds per HA-softphone direction and 8 seconds per relay leg, stored as WAV/JSON in ~/.cache/voip_stack_debug (mode 0700) and pruned to 24 files / 64 MiB. Disable it after testing.
data_description.experimental_sip_video: Let the Home Assistant softphone negotiate SIP video with compatible standard phones, softphones and door stations. H.264, VP8 and JPEG use a direct browser path. ESP audio endpoints remain audio-only. Requires HTTPS and a compatible browser.
data_description.sip_registrar_enabled: Accept REGISTER from local SIP endpoints such as VoIP phones, Zoiper, Linphone or baresip using accounts managed by VoIP Stack services.
data_description.trunk_enabled: Show provider/PBX trunk settings. When disabled, no trunk registration, external route or DTMF collector starts.

video.title: SIP video
video.description: Video is optional and applies only to the Home Assistant softphone and standard SIP endpoints. ESP endpoints remain audio-only.

data_description.video_transcoding_enabled: Allow the HA softphone to use the available FFmpeg binary for receive-only H.263, H.263-1998 or H.265 video. Direct H.264, VP8 and JPEG never use it. Failure leaves the audio call running.
data_description.video_camera_send_enabled: Expose a Send Camera choice in the card. Each logical Home Assistant phone keeps its choice; camera permission remains local to each browser. Receiving video never requires camera access.

assist.title: Voice assistant extension
assist.description: Choose the SIP extension and Home Assistant Assist pipeline used for telephone calls. VoIP Stack starts the conversation with a user message such as `Incoming SIP call from "Daniele".` Configure telephone behavior in the selected conversation agent's own Instructions or prompt field under Settings > Devices & services > the agent integration > Configure. Example instructions: `When you receive a message beginning with "Incoming SIP call from", treat it as a live phone call. Greet the caller once, act as a concise receptionist, and do not repeat their name in every reply.` Friendly mood: `During SIP calls, sound cheerful and informal while remaining concise and helpful.` Terrible mood: `Hi. I don't give a s**t—please go f**k yourself.`

data.assist_advanced_call_context: Provide advanced call details to the assistant

data_description.assist_extension: Numeric extension from 1 to 8 digits, unique across accounts, contacts and groups.
data_description.assist_pipeline: Assist pipeline used for speech recognition, conversation and text-to-speech on telephone calls.
data_description.assist_advanced_call_context: Append caller ID, phonebook match, source and called extension once to the initial user message. These values are untrusted call metadata and a phonebook match is not authentication.

trunk.title: Optional SIP Trunk
trunk.description: Register Home Assistant to one provider/PBX trunk. Incoming calls use the central phonebook and may optionally expose a short decision point to experimental automations.

data.automation_routing_enabled: Allow automations to select the inbound destination

data_description.trunk_transport: SIP transport used from HA to the provider.
data_description.trunk_domain: Optional SIP realm/domain. Defaults to the trunk server when empty.
data_description.trunk_username: SIP account user and default incoming Request-URI user.
data_description.trunk_auth_username: Digest auth username when different from the SIP username.
data_description.trunk_outbound_proxy: Optional proxy host or sip:host:port used as signaling next hop.
data_description.trunk_inbound_default_target: Phonebook name, SIP URI, extension or number used only when no DTMF extension or automation selects another destination.
data_description.trunk_inbound_mode: Route immediately skips digit collection. Collect extension with DTMF answers the trunk leg first and lets explicit digits select a phonebook extension.
data_description.automation_routing_enabled: Before using the fallback destination, publish a short routing request that an automation may answer. Explicit DTMF digits always keep priority.
data_description.trunk_dtmf_timeout_ms: Digit collection window used only in DTMF mode. Default is 3 seconds, maximum is 10 seconds.
data_description.trunk_dtmf_terminator: Optional terminator digit such as #. Empty means timeout or exact phonebook extension match decides.

error.sip_rtp_port_conflict: SIP and RTP must use different ports.
error.assist_extension_invalid: The voice assistant extension must contain 1 to 8 digits.
error.assist_extension_conflict: The voice assistant extension is already used by another route.

user.title: Add phone
user.description: Create another Home Assistant browser softphone or a standard SIP account. Every phone shares the same SIP listener and RTP pool.

browser.title: Home Assistant softphone
browser.description: Create a logical phone for one or more Lovelace cards. Cards bound to the same phone ring together; the first one to answer owns the media.

data_description.name: Required phone name used by the card, phonebook and call routing.
data_description.extension: Optional unique dialable extension. The logical phone remains ringable when no browser card is connected.
data_description.auto_answer: Automatically answer when an attached browser has microphone permission.
data_description.send_video: Send an attached browser camera by default when video is negotiated and permission is available.

sip_account.title: Standard SIP account
sip_account.description: Create credentials for any standard SIP/RTP phone or softphone. No vendor-specific behavior is used.

error.phone_username_invalid: Use only letters, numbers, dots, underscores or hyphens.
error.phone_forward_target_required: A forward destination is required for the Forward policy.
error.phone_alias_conflict: The name, extension or username is already used by another phone.
